revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''高根横穴墓群'''（たかねおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]の[[遺跡]]...'
revision.text:
'''高根横穴墓群'''（たかねおうけつぼぐん）は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]にある[[古墳時代]]の[[横穴墓|横穴墓群]]の[[遺跡]]。高根山北面の山頂付近、標高約89メートルの地点に凝灰岩の岩盤をくりぬいて構築された。現在1基（高根山1号墓）が開口しているが、周辺にはまだ数基ほど埋没していると思われる。平成9年（1997）1月20日付けで江南町（当時）の史跡に指定された。
*高根山1号墓 :開口部の幅2.5メートル、高さ1.5メートル、奥行き4メートル。遺物は未調査であり、知られていない。
== 参考文献 == 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 大里』 さきたま出版会 2004年
== 関連事項 == * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

*高根山1号墓 :開口部の幅2.5メートル、高さ1.5メートル、奥行き4メートル。遺物は未調査であり、知られていない。

revision.comment: Created page with " == Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. === Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site..."
revision.text:
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi Tombs == These tombs are found from Kyushu through San'in, Kinki, Hokuriku, and Tokai, with a high concentration in [[Southern Kantō]]. The northern limit is thought to be in northern [[Miyagi Prefecture]]. Approximately 3,000 examples exist in [[Shizuoka Prefecture]].
=== Tōhoku Region === * {{ill|Yamahata Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|山畑横穴群}} ({{ill|Ōsaki, Miyagi|ja|大崎市}}, [[Miyagi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 26 tombs * {{ill|Atagoyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|愛宕山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Taihaku-ku, Sendai|ja|太白区}}, {{ill|Sendai|ja|仙台市}}) * {{ill|Mogasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|茂ヶ崎横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) - 25 tombs, no longer extant * {{ill|Sōzenji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宗禅寺横穴墓群}} (Taihaku-ku, Sendai) * {{ill|Kiyotosaku Yokoguchi|ja|清戸迫横穴墓}} ({{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|ja|双葉町}}, [[Fukushima Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Izumizaki Yokoguchi|ja|泉崎横穴墓}} ({{ill|Nishishirakawa District|ja|西白河郡}}, {{ill|Izumizaki, Fukushima|ja|泉崎村}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nakada Yokoguchi|ja|中田横穴}} ({{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Hayama Yokoguchi|ja|羽山横穴}} ({{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Wada Daibutsu and Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|和田大仏及び横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sukagawa|ja|須賀川市}}, Fukushima Prefecture) - Municipal Historic Site
=== Kantō Region === * {{ill|Jūgorō-ana Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十五郎穴横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hitachinaka, Ibaraki|ja|ひたちなか市}}, [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]) - Over 300 tombs * {{ill|Karagoshō Yokoguchi|ja|唐御所横穴}} ({{ill|Nasukaragawa, Tochigi|ja|那珂川町}}, [[Tochigi Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, part of the Hokōda and Wami Yokoguchi Clusters * {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakka|ja|長岡百穴古墳}} ({{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) - 52 tombs * {{ill|Yoshimi Hyakka|ja|吉見百穴}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|ja|吉見町}}, [[Saitama Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Kuroiwa Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|黒岩横穴墓群}} (Yoshimi, Saitama) - Over 30 tombs * {{ill|Jūrō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十郎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Hatoyama, Saitama|ja|鳩山町}}, Saitama) - 3 tombs * {{ill|Takinojō Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|滝之城横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tokorozawa|ja|所沢市}}, Saitama) - 9 tombs * {{ill|Kitaakitsu Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北秋津横穴墓群}} (Tokorozawa, Saitama) - 8 tombs * {{ill|Bikuniyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|比丘尼山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Higashimatsuyama|ja|東松山市}}, Saitama) - Over 50 tombs * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高根横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|長柄横穴群}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|ja|長柄町}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|出山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|ja|三鷹市}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|中和田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|谷沢川横穴群}} ({{ill|Setagaya|ja|世田谷区}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|荏子田横穴}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|ja|青葉区}}, {{ill|Yokohama|ja|横浜市}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|市ヶ尾横穴古墳群}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|松ヶ崎横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|ja|港南区}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|ja|栄区}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|宮ノ前横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|柏谷横穴群}} ({{ill|Kannami|ja|函南町}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|北江間横穴群}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|ja|伊豆の国市}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|江浦横穴群}} ({{ill|Numazu|ja|沼津市}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|ja|法皇山横穴古墳}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|ja|加賀市}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高井田横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|ja|柏原市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|安福寺横穴群}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|十王免横穴群}} ({{ill|Matsue|ja|松江市}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|狐谷横穴墓群}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|上塩冶横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|高津久横穴墓群}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|ja|知夫村}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|ja|竹並遺跡}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|ja|行橋市}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|城山横穴群}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|ja|福智町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|ja|水町遺跡}} ({{ill|Nōgata|ja|直方市}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|ja|古月横穴}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|ja|鞍手町}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫ナギノ横穴群}} ({{ill|Tamana|ja|玉名市}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|石貫穴観音横穴群}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|鍋田横穴群}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|ja|山鹿市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|大村横穴群}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|ja|人吉市}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|四日市横穴群}} ({{ill|Usa|ja|宇佐市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|ja|滝尾百穴横穴古墳群}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|ja|蓮ヶ池横穴群}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|ja|宮崎市}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Kanazawa River Basin Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichishiyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaoka Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Yokoguchi Kofun|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasunogaike Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.
*

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* **Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1** * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]
== Distribution and Notable Yokoguchi TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|Eura Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Yokoguchi|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Yokoguchi Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Yokoguchi Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Yokohama Tombs.

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Eura Cave Tomb Cluster" to "E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. revision.text:
'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Yokouana Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Yokouana Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Yokouana Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Fixing the kk issue]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kumagaya|en}}[[ja:熊谷市]], Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:長柄横穴群]] ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en}}[[ja:長柄町]], [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:出山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:三鷹市]], [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:中和田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:多摩市]], Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:谷沢川横穴群]] ({{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:荏子田横穴]] ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:青葉区]], {{ill|Yokohama|en}}[[ja:横浜市]], [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:市ヶ尾横穴古墳群]] (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:㹨川流域横穴墓群]] (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:松ヶ崎横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:港南区]], Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en}}[[ja:七石山横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}[[ja:栄区]], Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:宮ノ前横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:ひこしヶ谷横穴墓群]] (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en}}[[ja:柏谷横穴群]] ({{ill|Kannami|en}}[[ja:函南町]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:北江間横穴群]] ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}[[ja:伊豆の国市]], Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:江浦横穴群]] ({{ill|Numazu|en}}[[ja:沼津市]], Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en}}[[ja:法皇山横穴古墳]] ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}[[ja:加賀市]], [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en}}[[ja:高井田横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}[[ja:柏原市]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:安福寺横穴群]] (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:十王免横穴群]] ({{ill|Matsue|en}}[[ja:松江市]], [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:狐谷横穴墓群]] (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:上塩冶横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}[[ja:出雲市]], Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:高津久横穴墓群]] ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}[[ja:知夫村]], [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en}}[[ja:竹並遺跡]] ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}[[ja:行橋市]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:城山横穴群]] ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福智町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en}}[[ja:水町遺跡]] ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}[[ja:直方市]], Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en}}[[ja:古月横穴]] ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:鞍手町]], Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫ナギノ横穴群]] ({{ill|Tamana|en}}[[ja:玉名市]], [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:石貫穴観音横穴群]] (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:鍋田横穴群]] ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en}}[[ja:山鹿市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:大村横穴群]] ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en}}[[ja:人吉市]], Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}}[[ja:四日市横穴群]] ({{ill|Usa|en}}[[ja:宇佐市]], [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:滝尾百穴横穴古墳群]] ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en}}[[ja:大分市]], Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:蓮ヶ池横穴群]] ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en}}[[ja:宮崎市]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Ichigao Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster" to "Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster"
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' * Entrance width: 2.5 meters * Entrance height: 1.5 meters * Depth: 4 meters * No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
{{DEFAULTSORT:Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group}} [[Category:Clustered tombs]] [[Category:Ancient tombs in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya City]]

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
