'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=draft}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=draft}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum
== Distribution and Notable Cave Tomb TombsSaitama) - Over 50 tombs == * {{ill|Takane Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kumagaya|en|12=draft}}, Saitama) - Municipal Historic Site * {{ill|Nagara Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Nagara, Chiba|en|12=draft}}, [[Chiba Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 300 tombs * {{ill|Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Mitaka, Tokyo|en|12=draft}}, [[Tokyo]]) - Metropolitan Historic Site, 10 tombs * {{ill|Nakawada Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo) - 14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|12=draft}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en}}, [[Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|12=draft}}, Yokohama) - Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * {{ill|Kashiya Cave Tombs|ja|柏谷横穴群|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kannami|en}}, [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Numazu|en}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs
=== Hokuriku Region === * {{ill|Hōōzan Cave Tombs||ja|法皇山横穴古墳|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kaga, Ishikawa|en}}, [[Ishikawa Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 150 tombs
=== Kinki Region === * {{ill|Takaida Cave Tombs|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kashiwara, Osaka|en}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Anfukuji Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Kashiwara, Osaka) - 32 tombs
=== Chūgoku Region === * {{ill|Jūōmen Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Matsue|en|12=draft}}, [[Shimane Prefecture]]) - 37 tombs * {{ill|Kitsunedani Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} (Matsue, Shimane) - 16 tombs * {{ill|Kamikonishi Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en}}, Shimane) * {{ill|Takatsuku Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Chibu, Shimane|en}}, [[Oki District]])
=== Kyushu Region === * {{ill|Takenami Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yukuhashi|en}}, [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]) - Over 1,000 tombs * {{ill|Shiroyama Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site, over 200 tombs * {{ill|Mizumachi Site|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Nōgata|en}}, Fukuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, over 40 tombs * {{ill|Kozuki Cave Tomb|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kurate, Fukuoka|en}}, Fukuoka) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Nagino Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} ({{ill|Tamana|en}}, [[Kumamoto Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Ishinuki Anakanon Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=draft}} (Tamana, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site * {{ill|Nabeta Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Yamaga, Kumamoto|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 60 tombs * {{ill|Ōmura Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Hitoyoshi|en|12=draft}}, Kumamoto) - National Historic Site, 27 tombs * {{ill|Yokkaichi Cave Tomb Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Usa|en|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 161 tombs * {{ill|Takio Hyakka Cave Tomb Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Ōita, Ōita|en|12=draft}}, Ōita) - Municipal Historic Site, 75 tombs * {{ill|Hasugaike Kofun Cluster|en}} ({{ill|Miyazaki, Miyazaki|en|12=draft}}, [[Miyazaki Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site
== Related Articles == * [[Kofun period]] * [[Decorated kofun|Decorated Kofun]]
== References == * Nagahara, Keiji (1999). Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary. Iwanami Shoten. * Yokohama History Foundation (2013). History of Cave Tombs.
*

revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster-->

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Deyama Cave Tomb Cluster]] → [[Deyama Graves]]
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:埼玉県の古墳
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]]

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: replace redirect category → Kofun of Saitama prefecture
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Clustered tombs
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Kumagaya City
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun template and pages that transclude it
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

Municipal Historic Site, 8 tombs ** {{ill|Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|ja|七石山横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shichisekiyama Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Sakae-ku, Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakae-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya, Saitama]] [[Category:群集墳]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

14 tombs * {{ill|Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Todoroki Ravine Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo) * {{ill|Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekoda Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Aoba-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoba-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, {{ill|Yokohama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama|qq=}}, [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]) - Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - 12 tombs
=== Tokai Region === * [[:en:Kashiya Cave Tombs|Kashiya Cave Tombs]] ({{ill|Kannami|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannami|qq=}}, [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]) - National Historic Site, 106 tombs * {{ill|Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaema Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Izunokuni|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izunokuni|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - National Historic Site, 101 tombs * {{ill|E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-no-ura Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Numazu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Numazu|qq=}}, Shizuoka) - Prefectural Historic Site, 92 tombs

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya, Saitama]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya, Saitama]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Takane Cave Tomb Cluster]]

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

Municipal Historic Site, 2 tombs * {{ill|Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichigao Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Aoba-ku, Yokohama) - Prefectural Historic Site, 19 tombs * {{ill|Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|ja|㹨川流域横穴墓群|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itachigawa Basin Rock-Cut Tombs|qq=}} (Kamakura-style tomb concentration) ** {{ill|Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsugasaki Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} ({{ill|Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnan-ku, Yokohama|qq=}}, Yokohama) - Over 100 tombs originally ** {{ill|Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyanomae Tunnel Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 23 tombs ** {{ill|Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoshigaya Cave Tomb Cluster|qq=}} (Sakae-ku, Yokohama) - 12 tombs

<!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--><!--Takane Cave Tomb Cluster--> [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya, Saitama]] [[Category:Gunshūfun]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17229037}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kumagaya-bunkazai.jp/museum/jousetu/bunkazai/281bunka.htm Takane Cave Tomb Tomb] - Kumagaya Digital Museum

12 tombs

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:高根横穴墓群]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|36.09605555555556|N|139.34105555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q17229037]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Takane Cave Tomb Tomb Group''' (高根横穴墓群) is an archaeological site consisting of rock-cut tombs from the [[Kofun period]] located in [[Kumagaya City]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. These tombs were carved into a tuff rock formation near the summit of Takane Mountain's northern slope, at an altitude of approximately 89 meters. While only one tomb (Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1) is currently open, it is believed that several other tombs remain buried in the surrounding area. The site was designated as a Historic Site of [[Kōnan Town]] on January 20, 1997.
* '''Takane Mountain Tomb No. 1''' ** Entrance width: 2.5 meters ** Entrance height: 1.5 meters ** Depth: 4 meters ** No artifacts have been investigated or identified from this tomb.
== References == * Hiroshi Shiono, *Saitama no Kofun: Osato*, Sakitama Publishing, 2004. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
